string 2:
Disturbance detected above the government building.
Public safety is... Their weapon has been deployed...

...with Shota Kuroi piloting. It appears to be headed for
the government building. We were... too late...!
I won't allow it. Such an ugly machine...

I quit because I couldn't stand the idea of being forced to
make something like that.
The Dr. Yuki I respect would never.
Dr. Yuki is the man who adopted me.{next}
You seem to know a lot about my adoptive father...{next}
He's my adoptive father... He disappeared on me.{next}

What are you talking about? If that's some sort of joke, it
went over my head.
Dr. Yuki is an anime character.
He's from a series I will always love, "Agent Alpha."

Don't tell me you haven't seen that anime? You're seriously
missing out.

Dr. Yuki disappears during an experiment, and his child,
the protagonist, goes looking for him.

That's the gist of the plot. But I can talk about that anime
for hours.

That series means so much to me. It's the reason I became a
scientist to begin with.
Hmm?
What's wrong? You don't look so good.
Truly deplorable... What would Dr. Yuki say if he existed?

While bound by Chronomon's rage... I provided various things to
the humans.

Digimon and their special structural data... all of which
went into the making of that weapon.

Kuga was an obedient little lapdog. Not a shred of pride in
that one, I'm afraid...

The weapon is beyond the ability of anyone to control now.
For you see...

...it, too, is now an incarnation of Chronomon's bitter rage.
The Digimon core was installed for this reason.

Chronomon's aim is, in the end, a clash of forces... The
space-time rift born from the war will open the sealed door.

And then you and Chronomon shall meet... The victims of this
conflict are but offerings to bring this about.

If war itself is the point... then Chronomon probably
doesn't care if it's Digimon or humans who are dying.

Destruction ad nauseum, all for a selfish purpose... If we
don't stop that weapon, so much blood will be spilled...

I will try to connect the gate to somewhere near the site.
Please let me know when you are ready.
Inori... Where the heck did you go?!
I've gone back over and over and over... but I still can't save you...!
I can't find a future where you're safe...!
It's like... the gods have made up their minds about you disappearing.

How many more times do I have to go back before I can see
your smile again...?

I won't give up... no matter how many times I fail. I made
you a promise, didn't I? A promise to protect you...!

Inori, Inori, Inori, Inori, Inori, Inori, Inori, Inori,
Inori, Inori, Inori!
Look out!
It's been a while.
I'm sure you've realized by now...
...realized you aren't a normal human being.
The one who created you must be quite satisfied.
You've done well to fulfill your role thus far.

To honor your achievements, allow me to tell you who you
are.

Since Aegiomon disappeared, you've been mistaken for 
the Great Guardian...
I'm sure you can understand why.

Your creator... is the higher being, that was challenged by
Chronomon to battle.
This higher being's name is Homeostasis.

Chronomon's plan has disrupted the equilibrium between
worlds and threatened their very existence.
You were given a role to eliminate anyone who upsets natural law.
The higher being chose a certain existence to act as a base when giving you life.
That being was Aegiomon, or the "dark shadow."

Aegiomon had been scattered across countless timelines
after traveling through time again and again.

Your existence is based on the dark shadow in one of
those timelines.
There is a clear reason why that method was used.

The higher being has a strong aversion to directly and
excessively intervening in the world it watches over.

To minimize its influence, it chose to take advantage of
these dark shadows.

What the higher being hoped for was a sort of "self-purifying
effect" on the world.

Aegiomon, who lost someone important to him and who was
driven by pain, disrupted the laws of time.
And thus the higher being tasked him with righting his own wrongs.

It took an instance of a dark shadow from one of these
timelines and crafted him into your form.

ADAMAS, your personality, your memories... were all prepared
for you to help you fulfill your mission efficiently.
That goes for me as well. I only exist in your head.

You are the agent in charge of maintaining the world's
equilibrium.

When establishing your character, we used some of the
memories of the dark shadow.
It was the wish of the dark shadow for us to do so.
The dark shadow's wish... In other words, it was Aegiomon's wish.
Aegiomon wanted to reunite Inori Misono with her little brother.
He tried to accomplish that by taking her brother's place.

Inori's little brother was a big fan of a certain character.
He often liked to pretend he was that character...
Your appearance, your memories...
They were created in part from the memories of Inori's little brother.

The reason you're being mistaken for Aegiomon is because
you were originally born from him.

As the time of integration approached, your form was
naturally returning to that of Aegiomon's.

Chronomon gave birth to Aegiomon. Aegiomon gave birth
to countless dark shadows.
And you were created from one of those dark shadows.

Like the cycle of time, equilibrium is maintained when all
things are connected.

Yes, all things in this world are nothing but gears of time.
However, that in itself is no cause for dismay.

What should cause alarm is when those gears go awry,
stopping the proper flow of time.

That is exactly what happened to those dark shadows...
to Aegiomon.
When time moves forward and we can progress onward...
...we can choose our future. That is where all our hopes lie.
I see. So that's how it is, huh...?
I get it now.
A me that lost it from losing Inori turned the world all crazy, huh.
That's... the truth behind the Shinjuku Inferno.
All of that happened because I have the power to alter time.

Me going back in time prevented time from moving forward.
The world's time just stopped.
I probably... never should've used this power of mine...
You came to help me, didn't you?

You came to encourage me, to tell me to move forward
and restart the flow of time.
But is that really what you want?
If I move forward and stop trying to repeat time...

...the dark shadows will disappear. That probably means
you'll cease to exist, too...
Why do you suppose I exist in this form?{next}
Don't you have someone you want to see again?{next}
That's the reason for everything that's happened so far.{next}
Okay...
I'm sorry...
but...
...thank you!
HAAAAAAAAAAAAAGH!
I'm sorry for keeping you waiting.
I need you to lend me—Jupitermon—your strength.
Let us take down Chronomon and move forward...
...forward into the future.
What a truly divine sight...!

I still remember your warmth when you were just an egg.
Being able to see you in this form has made it all worth it.
Chronomon is linked to that weapon the humans made.
...so it is trying to destroy the world's very foundation—time itself.

And when he—the Keeper of Time—disappears, his goal
will be complete.
So, in a sense, defeat is in fact what Chronomon truly seeks.
I think I understand a little of how Chronomon feels.
I felt the same way when I lost Inori.

I couldn't care less what happened to a world without Inori.
That's what most likely caused the Shinjuku Inferno.
But I can say this now. I was wrong.

Yet there was someone encouraging me. That bond
finally made me realize...
I can't just keep living in the past. I have to choose the future.
So let us defeat Chronomon and move forward!
